ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Petition For Final Distribution Or A Status Report.
Off Calendar Jul-06-2009 Off Calendar

Petition For Orders Directing Executor; Petition For Payment Of Attorney'S Fees From Estate Under Common Fund Theory
Off Calendar Mar-23-2009 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Espey for Mimi Moungovan. Megan Holbrook for Stephen Penkowsky. Robert Sehr for the Dutra Family Trust.

Ms. Espey stated that her client had requested information, that she only received partial information, that she and the executor have a difference of opinion about what is reasonable information, that the property is still in escrow, that the accountings show unexplained reimbursements, and that it is not prudent to allow the trustee to slide. Ms. Holbrook stated that the concerns were answered by Leroy Dutra in his declaration that she filed, that they have protected the assets, that Mr. Sehr agreed to do an additional accounting, that there is an agreement to toll the statute of limitations, that no distributions will be done until they are satisfied with the accountings, and that Ms. Espey's efforts haven't shown a benefit to the estate. Mr. Sehr explained that the accounting issues were due to Leroy Dutra not being a bookkeeper, that he kept records in an account book, that a CPA would then prepare the accounting with that information, that they requested receipts from him, and that there is $12,000 which Mr. Dutra spent and hasn't been reimbursed. In addition, he addressed the reason for the lot line adjustment. Ms. Espey presented additional argument to support her client's position. The parties submitted. The Court denied the petition without prejudice and requested Ms. Holbrook prepare the order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-09-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Megan Holbrook for and with Stephen Penkowsky. Janet Espey and Carolyn Espey for and with Mimi Moungovan (Beneficiary and Objector). Robert Sehr for the Dutra Trust.

Ms. Holbrook stated that she had filed a response to Ms. Moungovan's objection, reported that there is a piece of property that has not sold, and noted that an accounting has been filed. Mr. Sehr stated that the estimated date to close escrow is March 31, 2009, that he was waiting on a bond issue, and once that is resolved it will close. The Court stated that there were two petition's attached to the opposition, that they were not properly noticed, that proper notice needs to be given, that it was satisfied with the contents of the report, that the sale of the Brentwood property was holding up the close of the estate, and that it was inclined to continued the matter. Janet Espey stated that there appear to be discrepancies with distributions that were made, that the trustee wasn't forthcoming with information, and that the report is not complete. To recap, the Court stated that Ms. Espey believed that there are assets due to the estate, and that there are missing details to the report. The Court approved the status report (subject to review of the order), and continued the matter to July 6, 2009 for the filing of the petition for final distribution or a current status report.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jackie Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Brian Rice.

Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-15-2008 Continued To Jan-05-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
